channel.title: NPR Topics: World
channel.description: NPR world news, international art and culture, world business and financial markets, world economy, and global trends in health, science and technology. Subscribe to the World Story of the Day podcast and RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Bolivia's capital under siege as protests deepen crisis for President Paz
item.description: Bolivia's President Rodrigo Paz faces a deepening crisis as widespread protests and blockades leave the political capital under siege less than six months after he took office.
item.content:encoded:
Bolivia's President Rodrigo Paz faces a deepening crisis as widespread protests and blockades leave the political capital under siege less than six months after he took office.

(Image credit: Freddy Barragan)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: WHO chief concerned over 'scale and speed' of Ebola outbreak
item.description: The World Health Organization top official has expressed concern over the rapid spread of a rare type of Ebola in Congo. Authorities have reported at least 134 suspected deaths and over 500 cases.
item.content:encoded:
The World Health Organization top official has expressed concern over the rapid spread of a rare type of Ebola in Congo. Authorities have reported at least 134 suspected deaths and over 500 cases.

(Image credit: Dirole Lotsima Dieudonne)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Xi and Putin meet to reaffirm China-Russia ties days after Trump's visit
item.description: Chinese leader Xi Jinping welcomed Russian President Vladimir Putin in Beijing on Wednesday in a meeting meant to reaffirm ties and that takes place only days after a visit by President Donald Trump.
item.content:encoded:
Chinese leader Xi Jinping welcomed Russian President Vladimir Putin in Beijing on Wednesday in a meeting meant to reaffirm ties and that takes place only days after a visit by President Donald Trump.

(Image credit: Maxim Shemetov)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: 'Taiwan Travelogue' wins the 2026 International Booker Prize
item.description: The novel is the first work translated from Mandarin Chinese to win the award, which celebrates its 10th anniversary this year.
item.content:encoded:
The novel is the first work translated from Mandarin Chinese to win the award, which celebrates its 10th anniversary this year.

(Image credit: Adrian Dennis)
item.dc:creator: Ivy Buck

item.title: Israel's trying to expel a whole Palestinian district in East Jerusalem, activists say
item.description: Israeli authorities are issuing Palestinians demolition orders in East Jerusalem at an accelerated rate since Israel launched war with Iran, human rights groups and U.N. experts say.
item.content:encoded:
Israeli authorities are issuing Palestinians demolition orders in East Jerusalem at an accelerated rate since Israel launched war with Iran, human rights groups and U.N. experts say.

(Image credit: Ammar Awad)
item.dc:creator: Eleanor Beardsley

item.title: California mosque shooting leaves 5 dead. And, judge dismisses Trump's IRS lawsuit
item.description: San Diego authorities are investigating a deadly shooting at a mosque as a hate crime. And, Trump dropped his lawsuit against the IRS, paving the way for an "anti-weaponization fund."
item.content:encoded:
San Diego authorities are investigating a deadly shooting at a mosque as a hate crime. And, Trump dropped his lawsuit against the IRS, paving the way for an "anti-weaponization fund."

(Image credit: Zoe Meyers)
item.dc:creator: Brittney Melton

item.title: The French Open courts are clay, a tricky surface for some. Here's how the pros do it
item.description: In tennis, clay has a reputation for being one of the harder surfaces to play on. But a few pros shared some of their tips for staying sharp.
item.content:encoded:
In tennis, clay has a reputation for being one of the harder surfaces to play on. But a few pros shared some of their tips for staying sharp.

(Image credit: Adam Pretty)
item.dc:creator: Ayana Archie

item.title: One Palestinian man's life of survival
item.description: A 90-year-old Palestinian man in Gaza says his life has never felt safe as he recalls displacements and wars
item.content:encoded: A 90-year-old Palestinian man in Gaza says his life has never felt safe as he recalls displacements and wars
item.dc:creator: Anas Baba

item.title: Putin visits China to reaffirm Russia ties, as Xi also seeks stable U.S. relations
item.description: The Kremlin has said Putin and Xi plan to discuss economic cooperation between the two countries, but also "key international and regional issues."
item.content:encoded:
The Kremlin has said Putin and Xi plan to discuss economic cooperation between the two countries, but also "key international and regional issues."

(Image credit: Alexander Kazakov)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Trump says he's called off Iran strike at request of Gulf allies
item.description: President Donald Trump says he is holding off on a military strike on Iran planned for Tuesday because "serious negotiations" are underway to end the war.
item.content:encoded:
President Donald Trump says he is holding off on a military strike on Iran planned for Tuesday because "serious negotiations" are underway to end the war.

(Image credit: Vahid Salemi)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
